title: Peppery Crunchy Uruguayan Pork Tenderloin Salad
description: A light, peppery salad featuring seared pork tenderloin, crisp veggies, and a zesty citrus dressing, inspired by Uruguayan flavors.

ingredients.name: pork tenderloin
ingredients.notes: trimmed and sliced into 1 cm strips

instructions: Season the pork strips with salt, pepper, smoked paprika, cumin, and oregano.
Heat 1 tbsp olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat and sear the pork for 2‑3 minutes per side until browned.
Add the remaining 1 tbsp olive oil, lemon zest, and a splash of lemon juice to the skillet, then pour in ½ cup water or broth.
Simmer the pork, covered, for 10–12 minutes or until the internal temperature reaches 145°F (63°C).
Remove the pork from the pan and let it rest for 5 minutes before slicing it into thin strips.
In a large bowl combine the sliced pork, red onion, carrot, bell pepper, tomatoes, cucumber, parsley, and cilantro.
Whisk together the dressing: 3 tbsp olive oil, 1 tbsp red wine vinegar, 1 tsp honey, lemon juice, salt, pepper, and optional crushed red pepper flakes.
Pour the dressing over the salad and toss gently to coat.
Taste and adjust seasoning if needed.
Serve immediately on a bed of mixed greens or with crusty bread if desired.
